Big Ideas in
3 Simple Steps
Dan Ferrari
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 61 --

What you’re going to get out of this…
• 	Sudden end to the desire to put your head
through a wall because you can’t find an idea
• 	Cut down on the time it takes you to develop
an idea
• 	Throw out the bad ideas before wasting time
on them
• 	Business Owners: dramatically improve the
odds that any offer you put out wins, and
wins BIG (even if you’re far removed from
the process)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 61 --

What you won’t get today…
• 	I’m not going to rehash the common (but useful) advice about
filling your brain, giving yourself space for the light bulbs to go
off, etc…
• 	That stuff IS critical, but you’ve heard it before.
• 	If not, read A Technique for Producing Ideas
• 	James Webb Young (legendary Mad Man)
• 	Less than 1 hour reading time
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 61 --

Can the Enormous Task of Creating
Big Ideas Really Be Broken Down into
Just 3 Steps?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 61 --

Yes! But it takes a bit of discipline 	–
you need follow the process
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 61 --

So what are these 3 simple steps…
First – there are only 2 components to a killer Big Idea.
Second – there are only 2 kinds of Big Ideas.
Third – Big Ideas don’t have to be guesses (this one’s worth 10x the price of admission!)
ALTERNATE PRESENTATION TITLE:
How to never again waste precious time and energy on shitty
ideas that have no chance of winning!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 61 --

Part One: Your Big Idea only needs
to check these 2 boxes…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 61 --

Your Big Idea only needs to check these 2
boxes…
• 	It must be RELEVANT
• 	It must be ELEGANT
THAT’S IT!!!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 61 --

“F-Off 	Dan! That sounds like a formula for
creating SMALL ideas!”
“What about urgency?”
“What about differentiation?”
“What about novelty?”
“HELP ME!"
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 61 --

Yes, all that’s important, but…
• 	In the early stages, you’re clawing around in the dark, you MUST keep it
simple
• 	All that stuff is either AUTOMATICALLY handled by making your idea as
elegant and relevant as possible…
• 	Or… you can add it as FINISHING TOUCHES once you refine your idea
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 61 --

OK, so then why are relevance and elegance so
important…
• 	Because the ONLY goal of a Big Idea is…
Provide your prospect with a vehicle
they 	CONNECT 	with and 	UNDERSTAND
INSTANTLY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 61 --

Relevance
• 	Something they can “unconsciously” relate to without a second thought
• 	Relevance doesn’t happen in isolation. Relevant ideas become irrelevant as
the market evolves and vice versa. Everything that happens in your
prospects’ world impacts what is relevant.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 61 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 61 --

What made this highly relevant?
• 	Written after Trump won the election.
• 	Lots of talk about legalizing sports betting
and gambling.
But the key was putting it in THEIR
backyard.
This forced them to immediately confront
how they felt about it, but through a new lens
of getting rich because of it.
Even more so, this period was insanely
disorienting for Baby Boomers. Someone who
could make sense of the world and all the
strange things happening was highly
appealing and therefore highly relevant.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 61 --

The bottom line: is this something that
will interest & connect 	with my market
on first glance?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 61 --

Next up, elegance
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 61 --

Elegance
• 	In my opinion, deserves more attention than relevance, because it’s
HARDER
• 	Big Ideas are NOT complex or inflated ideas (special warning to the “rising
stars”)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 61 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 61 --

The idea here is elegant and
straightforward, but completely novel
(and relevant to a huge mass
market)…
• 	Guys with great genetics & bodies
have 3 physiological advantages
• 	It’s easy for regular guys to trigger
these same physique-enhancing
changes in their bodies too
• 	All it takes is a new & unique form
of resistance training
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 61 --

The bottom line: is this something that
my market 	will understand 	on first
glance?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 61 --

Special Warning: Legal & Compliance
over-writes can impact elegance
Often what happens is you will have to include disclosures or add items
you omitted in the name of simplicity and this makes your idea to
complex and round-about
Work with your reviewers BEFOREHAND!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 61 --

So how do you know if you’ve “nailed” these
two criteria?
• 	The part I haven’t mentioned yet is elegance and relevance exist on a
spectrum
• 	They are opposites
• 	A good idea strikes a balance of these opposites
• 	A bad idea may achieve one, but it comes at the expense of the other
• 	There’s an easy litmus test you can do to judge if you’ve got a crap
idea…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 61 --

Introducing the
“Big Idea Sweet Spot”!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 61 --

ELEGANCE 	RELEVANCE
Big Idea
Sweet Spot
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 61 --

ELEGANCE 	RELEVANCE
You can try to radically simplify and condense an idea to
pure elegance, but if the idea is fundamentally flawed, doing
so will pull it away from relevance because you will have
stripped away anything that draws your prospect to the idea.
Big Idea
Sweet Spot
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 61 --

ELEGANCE 	RELEVANCE
Or you can try to throw in a lot of connections, false
devices, bait & switches, story tactics, and more to try to
make your idea more relevant to the prospect… that that
leads to bloat, complexity, and loss of “singular” focus ,
which pull your idea away from elegance.
Big Idea
Sweet Spot
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

ELEGANCE 	RELEVANCE
Big Idea
Sweet Spot

-- 28 of 61 --

Part Two: There are only 2 kinds of
Big Ideas…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 61 --

PROBLEM: Wading through the
limitless possibilities is really damn hard
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 61 --

There’s a simpler way…
• 	If elegance and relevance are the LENS through which
you look at Big Ideas, this next part will help you know
WHERE to look
• 	You either look deep INTO the product (Internal Ideas)
• 	Or… OUTWARD into the prospect’s world (External Ideas)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 61 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 61 --

Both of these promos are about using your
phone to make money in 2020…
• 	Yet… Rapid Income Engine is an INTERNAL IDEA
• 	It’s all about the trading software which can be operated entirely from your phone, and
the personal development story that brought it to life
• 	This idea is based primarily from what’s “inside” the product
• 	Inbound Closer is an EXTERNAL IDEA
• 	It’s all about how a booming industry (online education) has created an opportunity to
escape the 9-5 and make money from anywhere as long as you have a phone
• 	The “booming industry” is about something “bigger” than the product
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 61 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 61 --

Internally-Focused Ideas…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 61 --

When you have a product or concept that is
truly unique
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 61 --

When your market 	knows what 	they want to
achieve and you have a unique 	way 	to 	get 	there
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 61 --

When you’re in a “commoditized” product 	area
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 61 --

When you have a really good “direct” narrative
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 61 --

When you have a noteworthy spokesperson or
guru
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 61 --

Externally-Focused Ideas…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 41 of 61 --

When you have a NEW discovery or
opportunity
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 61 --

When you are “creating” a problem
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 61 --

When you have defined heroes and villains
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 61 --

When you have emotionally charged events and
cultural narratives
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 61 --

When you market has known interests and
“fetishes”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 61 --

Last: These are NOT mutually exclusive
- 	you can find viable ideas in BOTH
directions
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

If you do everything you’ve learned today,
and wind up with one solid internal idea
and one solid external idea (which both
pass the Sweet Spot test), you may just

Part Three: No more guesswork!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

You’ve got an idea that follows the
first two steps, now what?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 50 of 61 --

Here’s what amateurs do…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 51 of 61 --

Here’s what minor leaguers do…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 52 of 61 --

Here’s what pros do…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 53 of 61 --

You run short “pilot” ads to see which
of your ideas gets the most engagement
Set up a simple traffic test (using the best “analog”
platform) and see which idea wins
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 54 of 61 --

Validate your ideas before you waste the time
writing a full promo
• 	There are MANY ways to do this
• 	The key is to duplicate format, audience, and traffic source as closely as possible
and let it run for several days (like any test)
• 	Evaluate CTR (or opt-in in certain cases)
• 	Test either as a split among potential ideas, or with just one idea against “par”
for whatever that space is
• 	Where do you send the traffic? Doesn’t really matter – you’re not trying to make
money, you’re saving yourself from wasting it (hard for businesses to swallow)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
